title: Operation entry — activator definition (server compute)
description: One row in <atlas>/registries/data/operations.registry.json. Defines how to call an atlas backend endpoint (e.g. atlas_server.py) that produces a result the registry can serve. The activator counterpart to a 'source: file' layer entry: a 'source: operation' layer references one of these by name through its 'operation' field. See ACTIVATOR_EXTRACTOR.md for the conceptual mapping and core/registry_core.schema.json (definitions.operation_entry) for the mirrored inline definition used by the runtime meta-schema. This file is the canonical version; the inline copy in core/ tracks it.

endpoint.description: HTTP path on the atlas backend server (e.g. '/popstats/region'). Concatenated with the server's base URL by OperationRunner.

method.description: HTTP method. POST is the default for compute endpoints (request body = JSON inputs). GET is for cheap pings or query-string endpoints.

inputs.description: Names of slots / args that get assembled into the request body (POST) or query string (GET). The runner first checks args[name], then falls back to AtlasState.shared[name] and per-atlas buckets.

output_schema.description: Path to the JSON Schema that validates the response payload. Validation is soft — if the schema file resolves and is not flagged _status: 'pending', a minimal type + required-keys check runs. See OperationRunner._validateSoft in core/operation_runner.js.

cache_key.description: Template with {slot} substitution used to key the response in the registry's cache. Distinct argument combinations must produce distinct keys.

cache_tier.description: Cache tier the response lives in (hot=RAM, warm=IndexedDB, cold=re-fetch each call). Most compute operations are warm.

estimated_latency_ms.description: Informational; lets the shell decide whether to show a spinner or pre-warm. Not enforced.

engine.description: Informational reference to the C engine binary that backs this operation server-side (region_popstats, hobs_windower, angsd, instant_q, ...). The actual binary path comes from master_config.engines.<name>.

query_params.description: Only meaningful for method=GET. Map of query-string parameter name to (optional) description. Values are filled from args / AtlasState at call time. See core/operation_runner.js _buildUrl.
